description: county seat of San Joaquin County, California, United States
description_wiki: Stockton is a city in and the county seat of San Joaquin County, California, United States.

description: severe economic crisis in the USA in the 1930s
description_wiki: In the United States, the Great Depression is commonly (though not universally) dated to the Wall Street crash of October 1929.

surface_forms: they elected Roosevelt as the President
When Roosevelt came into his offices
description: president of the United States from 1933 to 1945 (1882–1945)
description_wiki: Franklin Delano Roosevelt (January 30, 1882 – April 12, 1945), also known as FDR, was the 32nd president of the United States, serving from 1933 until his death in 1945.

description: United States government New Deal agency (1935–1939) employing millions of unemployed people to carry out public works projects
description_wiki: The Works Progress Administration (WPA; from 1935 to 1939, then known as the Work Projects Administration from 1939 to 1943) was an American New Deal agency that employed millions of jobseekers (mostly men who were not formally educated) to carry out public works projects, including the construction of public buildings and roads.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Oklahoma (  OH-klə-HOH-mə; Choctaw: Oklahumma, pronounced [oklahómma]) is a landlocked state in the South Central and Southwestern region of the United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Arkansas ( , AR-kən-saw) is a landlocked state in the West South Central region of the Southern United States.
The Grapes of Wrath is a 1940 American drama film directed by John Ford.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.
The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) is the domestic intelligence and security service of the United States and its principal federal law enforcement agency.

Manchuria is a historical region in Northeast Asia encompassing the entirety of present-day Northeast China and parts of the modern-day Russian Far East south of the Uda River and the Tukuringra-Dzhagdy ranges.

description: United States of America government agency
description_wiki: The War Production Board (WPB) was an agency of the United States government that supervised war production during World War II. President Franklin D. Roosevelt established it in January 1942, with Executive Order 9024.
Hiroshima Prefecture (広島県, Hiroshima-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [çi.ɾo.ɕi.ma, -maꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located in the Chūgoku region of Honshu.

Sumo (Japanese: 相撲, Hepburn: sumō; Japanese pronunciation: [ˈsɯmoː], lit. 'striking one another') is a form of competitive full-contact wrestling where a rikishi (wrestler) attempts to force his opponent out of a circular ring (dohyō) or into touching the ground with any body part other than the soles of his feet (usually by throwing, shoving or pushing him down).

description: municipal park in the Los Feliz neighborhood of Los Angeles, California, United States
description_wiki: Griffith Park is a large municipal park at the eastern end of the Santa Monica Mountains, ranging from the Los Feliz neighborhood of Los Angeles, California to the San Fernando Valley.

description: open-air theatre in Hollywood, Los Angeles, California, USA
description_wiki: The Hollywood Bowl is an amphitheatre and public park in the Hollywood Hills of Los Angeles, California.

description: Japanese language school in San Francisco, California
description_wiki: The Kinmon Gakuen (金門学園) or Golden Gate Institute is a Japanese language school in San Francisco, California, located at 2031 Bush Street.
